name: Roasted Habanero Salsa from Hell
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the tablespoon of olive oil and garlic, mixing well.
2. Rub the tomato halves with this mixture, sprinkle with salt and freshly cracked pepper.
3. Roast in a 500-degree oven until the tomatoes begin to take on some serious color, about 15 to 20 minutes. Remove from oven and let cool to room temperature before dicing.
4. Meanwhile, grill the habanero peppers over a medium-hot fire until slightly colored, about 2 to 3 minutes. Remove from heat and mince carefully.
5. Be very careful when working with these peppers. Wear gloves while mincing and wash any capsaicin on your skin with a mild bleach solution. Avoid rubbing your eyes and wash hands thoroughly afterward.
6. In a medium-sized bowl, combine the diced tomatoes, minced habanero peppers, 1/4 cup olive oil, lime juice, and chopped cilantro. Mix well.
7. Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 5 to 6 weeks. This salsa should remain free from mold or bacterial contamination.
